task.objective: Create a new BranchSelector component that displays branches with their statistics (pass rate, last run, execution count) and enables quick branch selection for the branch-first comparison workflow. This component is simpler than ExecutionSelector and focused on selecting branches rather than individual executions.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - Compare Page Enhancement
context.background:
The branch-first comparison workflow requires users to select two branches (baseline and current) rather than individual executions. The system then automatically uses the latest execution from each branch for comparison.
The existing `getBranches()` function already returns statistics for each branch: - branch: string (branch name) - last_run: string | null (ISO timestamp of most recent execution) - execution_count: number (total executions on this branch) - pass_rate: number (0-100 percentage) - last_status: "success" | "failure" | "running" | null
This data is already available via the `/api/executions` endpoint which returns `branches` (names only) and the full `branchStats` can be exposed.
NOTE: The BranchStatistics type already exists in lib/types.ts
context.audience: - Dashboard users comparing branches - Developers wanting quick "compare my branch vs main" workflow
context.impact: MEDIUM - Provides the UI foundation for branch-first comparison mode

existing_code.file: export interface BranchStatistics { branch: string last_run: string | null execution_count: number pass_rate: number last_status: "success" | "failure" | "running" | null }

functional.requirement: Create searchable dropdown for branch selection
Display pass rate as colored badge (green ≥80%, yellow 50-79%, red <50%)
Show last run timestamp formatted as relative time (e.g., "2 hours ago")
Support keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter to select)
Highlight "main" or "master" branch as primary option
Add "Quick compare with main" shortcut button

non_functional.requirement: Match glass-card aesthetic of existing components
Filter instantly on client-side (branches are already loaded)
Memoize filtering and sorting for performance

solution.approach: 1. Create a new BranchSelector component similar to ExecutionSelector 2. Import BranchStatistics type from lib/types.ts 3. Accept branches array with statistics as prop 4. Use date-fns formatDistanceToNow for relative time 5. Memoize filtered and sorted branches 6. Add ARIA attributes for accessibility 7. Add keyboard navigation support

steps.step: 1. Import BranchSelector into the compare page
4. Verify pass rate badges have correct colors (green/yellow/red)
5. Verify "main" or "master" appears first with "default" badge
6. Verify last run shows relative time format ("2 hours ago")
7. Verify status dot colors match branch status
4. Clear search, verify all branches return
1. Tab to selector, press Enter to open
3. Verify highlighted item scrolls into view
5. Press Escape to close without selecting

always.rule: Show "main" or "master" branch as primary option at top
Display pass rate with appropriate color coding
Format timestamps as human-readable relative time

never.rule: Never redefine types that already exist in lib/types.ts
Never make the dropdown difficult to close

output_format.structure: Create new file: `components/dashboard/branch-selector.tsx` The component is self-contained and reusable.
output_format.validation: After implementation: 1. Import and render in compare page 2. Verify styling matches ExecutionSelector 3. Test search and selection functionality 4. Test keyboard navigation 5. Run with screen reader to verify accessibility
